name: GameCube Controller
description: Nintendo's iconic controller for the GameCube console, featuring an asymmetric button layout with large A button, analog triggers, and a unique C-stick.

name: GameCube WaveBird
description: The wireless variant of the GameCube controller using 2.4 GHz RF. One of the first mainstream wireless controllers, sacrificing rumble for freedom of movement.

name: Switch Pro Controller
description: Nintendo's premium controller for the Switch console, featuring Bluetooth connectivity, HD rumble, NFC for amiibo, and a 40-hour battery life.

name: Switch 2 Pro Controller
description: The next-gen Nintendo Switch 2 Pro Controller with improved HD rumble, mouse-like optical sensor on the right stick, and USB-C charging.

name: N64 Controller
description: Nintendo's trident-shaped controller for the Nintendo 64, featuring an analog stick, D-pad, Z-trigger, and expansion port for Rumble and Controller Paks.

name: SNES Controller
description: The Super Nintendo controller that introduced the diamond four-button face layout and shoulder buttons that became industry standard for decades.

name: NES Controller
description: The original Nintendo Entertainment System controller with its iconic rectangular design, D-pad, and two action buttons plus Start and Select.

name: Wii Classic Controller
description: A traditional-style controller for the Wii that connects through the Wii Remote, featuring dual analog sticks and a layout reminiscent of the SNES controller.

name: Wii Remote
description: Nintendo's revolutionary motion-sensing controller for the Wii, featuring an accelerometer, infrared pointer, and Bluetooth connectivity.

name: PS5 DualSense
description: Sony's flagship PS5 controller featuring adaptive triggers, haptic feedback, built-in microphone, and a create button. A generational leap in controller technology.

name: PS4 DualShock 4
description: Sony's PS4 controller with a touchpad, light bar, built-in speaker, and improved analog sticks. Widely used on PC thanks to native Steam support.

name: PS3 DualShock 3
description: Sony's PS3 controller that brought back rumble feedback via Sixaxis motion sensing. Uses Bluetooth and pressure-sensitive face buttons.

name: PS2 DualShock 2
description: The PlayStation 2 controller with pressure-sensitive buttons and improved analog sticks. One of the most-produced game controllers in history.

name: PS1 DualShock
description: The original DualShock for PlayStation 1, which introduced dual analog sticks and vibration feedback that became the standard for all future controllers.

name: Xbox Series X|S Controller
description: Microsoft's latest Xbox controller with a textured grip, Share button, USB-C, and cross-platform Bluetooth support. Compatible with Xbox, PC, and mobile.

name: Xbox One Controller
description: Microsoft's Xbox One controller featuring impulse triggers with haptic feedback, improved ergonomics, and broad PC compatibility via XInput.

name: Xbox 360 Controller
description: The definitive PC gaming controller for a decade. Its XInput protocol became the de facto standard for PC game controller support.

name: Xbox Original Controller
description: Microsoft's first console controller, nicknamed 'The Duke' for its large size. Later revised as the Controller S with a smaller form factor.

name: Xbox Adaptive Controller
description: An accessibility-focused controller with large programmable buttons and 19 jacks for connecting external switches, buttons, mounts, and joysticks.

name: Sidewinder DualStrike
description: An experimental split-body gamepad from Microsoft's Sidewinder line. The right half tilts for aiming — an early attempt at dual-analog FPS control on PC.

name: Sidewinder Game Commander
description: A programmable multi-button controller from Microsoft's Sidewinder line designed for real-time strategy games, featuring 20+ macro-capable buttons.

name: 8BitDo M30
description: A six-button wireless controller modeled after the Sega Genesis/Mega Drive pad, with Bluetooth and USB-C. Perfect for retro fighting games and shmups.

name: 8BitDo Neo Geo Controller
description: A licensed wireless Neo Geo controller by 8BitDo with a clicky joystick and four face buttons. Designed for the Neo Geo Mini and compatible with PC/Switch.

name: 8BitDo PCE Controller
description: A wireless controller inspired by the TurboGrafx-16/PC Engine pad with 2.4 GHz and Bluetooth connectivity. Compact two-button design with turbo switches.

name: 8BitDo Bluetooth Adapter
description: A USB Bluetooth adapter that allows wireless controllers to be used on Switch, PC, and retro consoles. Bridges Bluetooth controllers to USB HID.

name: Astro City Mini Controller
description: A USB controller bundled with the Sega Astro City Mini arcade cabinet. Features a classic six-button arcade layout with microswitches.

name: Sega Genesis / Mega Drive Controller
description: Sega's three-button controller for the Genesis/Mega Drive, later expanded to six buttons. The foundation for an entire generation of fighting game players.

name: Sega Saturn Controller
description: Widely regarded as one of the best D-pads ever made. The Saturn controller's six-button layout and rolling D-pad made it a fighting game favorite.

name: Google Stadia Controller
description: Google's Wi-Fi-enabled controller built for the Stadia streaming service. After Stadia shut down, a firmware update enabled Bluetooth HID mode.

name: Logitech Wingman
description: An early USB gamepad from Logitech's Wingman line, popular for PC gaming in the late 90s with its comfortable ergonomics and reliable D-pad.

name: Logitech F310
description: A budget wired USB gamepad with switchable XInput/DirectInput modes. A reliable and affordable choice for PC gaming with broad compatibility.

name: Logitech F710
description: The wireless version of Logitech's F-series gamepads using a 2.4 GHz USB receiver. Supports XInput and DirectInput with a physical switch.

name: Raphnet PCE Adapter
description: A USB adapter by Raphnet Technologies that lets you use original TurboGrafx-16/PC Engine controllers on PC and other USB hosts.

name: Generic HID Gamepad
description: A standard USB HID-compliant gamepad. Most third-party and unbranded controllers present as generic HID devices and are supported through the standard protocol.

name: Generic XInput Controller
description: Any controller using Microsoft's XInput protocol. XInput is the standard for Xbox-compatible controllers and provides a consistent button layout across devices.

name: Generic DirectInput Controller
description: Any controller using the legacy DirectInput protocol. While older than XInput, many specialty controllers and flight sticks still use DirectInput for its flexibility.

name: PS5 DualSense Edge
description: Sony's premium pro controller for PS5 with swappable stick modules, back buttons, adjustable trigger travel, and on-controller profile switching.

name: Xbox Elite Series 2
description: Microsoft's premium Xbox controller with adjustable tension thumbsticks, interchangeable paddles, hair trigger locks, and USB-C charging dock.

name: 8BitDo Pro 2
description: A versatile controller with back buttons, motion controls, and a companion app for remapping. Supports Switch, PC, Android, and Apple via Bluetooth.

name: 8BitDo SN30 Pro
description: A compact SNES-styled controller with dual analog sticks, motion controls, and Bluetooth. Popular for retro gaming on Switch and PC.

name: Nintendo Switch Joy-Con
description: Nintendo's detachable controllers for the Switch, usable individually or as a pair. Feature HD rumble, IR camera, NFC, and motion controls.

name: Steam Controller
description: Valve's unconventional controller with dual trackpads, HD haptics, and deep Steam Input configurability. Discontinued but beloved by its community.

name: Hyperkin Duke (Xbox "The Duke" Remake)
description: A faithful recreation of the original massive Xbox controller with a built-in OLED screen showing the Xbox boot animation. XInput compatible for modern use.

name: Retro-Bit Sega Saturn USB
description: An officially licensed Sega Saturn controller replica with USB connectivity. Faithfully recreates the Saturn's legendary D-pad for modern platforms.

name: Hori Fighting Commander
description: A six-button fighting game controller by Hori with a wide D-pad and no analog sticks. Designed specifically for 2D fighters on PS4, PS3, and PC.

name: PowerA Enhanced Wireless Controller
description: An officially licensed wireless Switch controller with motion controls and two mappable back buttons at a budget price. No NFC or HD rumble.

name: Razer Wolverine V2
description: Razer's wired Xbox/PC controller with mecha-tactile action buttons, extra remappable buttons, and hair trigger mode for competitive FPS gaming.

name: GuliKit KingKong Pro 2
description: A controller featuring Hall effect analog sticks immune to drift, with swappable joystick modules. Compatible with Switch, PC, and Android.
